title: leontodon paludosus
intro: Leontodon paludosus, also known as Marsh Hawk's-bit, is a perennial plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to wetland areas in Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as acting as a diuretic and a laxative. Leontodon paludosus can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its availability and use may vary depending on the region and local regulations.
